pages.text: Every mob in the world carries a second loot table — its $(flesh)Despoil$() drops: sinew, fangs, glands, marrow, the raw matter of biomancy. An ordinary kill turns one out now and again, the way a butchered animal yields the odd unexpected cut — but only now and again, a scrap here and there, never a yield you could plan a craft around.$(br2)To harvest in $(italic)earnest$(), you make the Despoil roll land every time.
pages.title: The Flesh Plunderer

pages.text: $(bold)And you most likely already can.$() In Novus the $(flesh)Despoil$() enchantment is baked into $(bold)every knife$() — the bio-knife, every tier of Farmer's Delight knife, every Tinkers' and modded knife the pack adds. A knife is a full despoil tool the moment it is made: nothing to enchant, nothing to equip.$(br2)A knife already earns its place in your kit — for butchering meat and cutting wild plants. That same blade quietly guarantees the Despoil roll on everything it kills. For most players the harvest is simply $(italic)on$() from the first day.
pages.title: The Knife in Your Hand

pages.text: The $(flesh)Flesh Plunderer$() is the $(italic)dedicated$() despoil tool. Held in $(bold)either hand$() — main or off — it guarantees the Despoil roll on every kill, just as a knife's baked enchantment does; it need not land the blow.$(br2)Since knives already carry Despoil, the Plunderer is rarely necessary — but it is there if you want a despoiler you are not also cutting cabbages with. It is brittle, and pillaging wears it fast: treat it as a consumable.

$(bold)Despoil as an enchantment.$() It is an ordinary enchantment in its own right — the same one the knives carry. Put it on a sword or an axe and that weapon's kills roll the full Despoil table too.$(br2)Useful if your main weapon is not a knife and you would rather not carry the Plunderer: enchant it once, and your everyday weapon does the harvesting for you.
pages.title: Despoil, the Enchantment

pages.text: $(bold)Why it matters.$() The Despoil drops are the entire input side of biomancy. Fangs and claws build your first $(l:3_machines/bio_forge)Bio-Forge$(); glands and marrow are read by the $(l:2_cradle/six_hungers)Cradle$(); fibres and fragments are what the machines spend. No harvest, no craft — so it is a quiet mercy that a knife covers it without your ever thinking about it.
pages.title: The Point of It
